result.summary: speech.googleapis.com exposes Google Cloud Speech-to-Text HTTP APIs for v1, v1p1beta1, and v2, plus gcloud CLI support documented for Speech-to-Text workflows; authentication is via Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access tokens or API keys for the HTTP APIs, and gcloud login for the CLI.
result.description: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text converts audio into text using Google’s speech recognition models. It offers versioned APIs and related tooling for transcription and speech-recognition workflows.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Create or select a Google Cloud project, enable Cloud Speech-to-Text, then obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token using Google Cloud authentication. The easiest local path is to follow [Authenticate for using REST](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/rest) or [Authenticate for using client libraries](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/client-libraries). For CLI use, sign in with [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/authenticate) or use a service account / Application Default Credentials as described in [Authentication methods at Google](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication). Speech discovery documents list the `cloud-platform` scope.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: In Google Cloud Console, create an API key following [Manage API keys](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys). Then use it as the `key` query parameter as described in [Use API keys to access APIs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys-use). The Speech discovery document says the `key` parameter is required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource names such as `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/recognizers/{recognizer}`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Speech-to-Text location in resource names; docs describe regional endpoints and `global`.

variables.name: recognizer
variables.description: Recognizer ID in recognize and related resource paths.

packages.runtimeHint: Install via gcloud SDK installers/Homebrew/snap as documented by Google Cloud SDK
